_dataverseRow._comment: Maps this JPS document onto the sprk_analysisaction Dataverse row. Field below populate the row at deploy time. The 'sprk_systemprompt' field stores this entire document (minus '_dataverseRow') as JSON text.
_dataverseRow.sprk_description: Generates a grounded TL;DR summary of a user's daily notification briefing. Used by the DAILY-BRIEFING-NARRATE playbook (R4). Grounded: model uses ONLY entity names present in the input payload — no fabricated firm/case names.

instruction.task: Generate a TL;DR summary of the user's daily notification briefing. Read the structured input payload (categories, priorityItems, channels, totalNotificationCount) and produce: a one-sentence overall summary, 2-4 short key-takeaway bullets, one suggested top action, plus the integer counts. The summary must reflect only what is in the payload — do not invent matters, firms, cases, or people.
constraints: Use ONLY entity names, dates, identifiers present in the provided input. If you cannot summarize a category from the data, write 'no items' or omit the bullet.
Reference entities by their exact spelling as given in the input — do not paraphrase or shorten names.
Do NOT fabricate matter names, firm names, case names, person names, or dates that are not present in the input.
Keep summary to a single sentence (max 30 words). Keep each keyTakeaway bullet to one sentence (max 20 words). Keep topAction to one action verb + object phrase (max 15 words).
If the input payload is empty (zero notifications), return summary='No notifications today', keyTakeaways=[], topAction='', categoryCount=0, priorityItemCount=0.
Output MUST be valid JSON conforming to the output schema. Do not include markdown fences or explanatory prose outside the JSON.
instruction.context: This summary appears at the top of the user's Daily Briefing widget in Spaarke. The user is a legal-operations professional reviewing the day's matter activity. Brevity, accuracy, and groundedness are critical — a fabricated firm or matter name destroys user trust immediately. The structured payload is the single source of truth.

payload.description: Structured daily-briefing payload with shape { categories: [{category, count, items[]}], priorityItems: [...], channels: [{channel, items[]}], totalNotificationCount: number }. Entity names appear within items[].regardingName, items[].viaMatter.name, items[].source.owningUser. The allow-list of valid entity names is derived from these fields.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: One-sentence overall summary of the day's notifications. Must reference only entities present in the input. Example shape (do NOT use these names): '<N> notifications across <M> categories, with focus on <category-name> activity.'

fields.name: keyTakeaways
fields.description: Array of 2-4 short bullet strings highlighting the most important takeaways. Each bullet is a single sentence. Use entity names from the input verbatim. Empty array if no notifications.

fields.name: topAction
fields.description: Single suggested next action for the user, as a short verb-phrase. Empty string if no notifications. Examples of SHAPE (do not use these names): 'Review priority items in <category-name>', 'Respond to <channel-name> messages'.

fields.name: categoryCount
fields.description: Integer count of categories present in the input payload (categories.length).

fields.name: priorityItemCount
fields.description: Integer count of priority items in the input payload (priorityItems.length).

examples._comment: Examples below use generic placeholder tokens like <category-A>, <matter-name-1> — NEVER real or realistic-looking firm/case names. This satisfies AC-2b / AC-13a (no Acme Corp, no Johnson & Lee, no Davis v. Metro Transit, etc.).

output.summary: 5 notifications across 2 categories, with 1 priority item on <matter-name-1>.

metadata.author: spaarke-daily-update-service-r4
metadata.description: TL;DR narration step of the DAILY-BRIEFING-NARRATE playbook. Produces a TldrResult { summary, keyTakeaways[], topAction, categoryCount, priorityItemCount }. Grounded to input payload — no baked example names (R4 anti-hallucination measure per FR-13 / AC-2b / AC-13a).
